canto_title: The Ascent from Hell and the Signs of the Fallen

scenes.summary: Virgil instructs Dante to leave the heavy soul behind and proceed alone, allowing the two guides to travel more lightly.

scenes.scene_name: The warning to watch the path
scenes.summary: Virgil tells Dante to look down at the ground to observe the symbolic markings of the deceased.
Dante observes the fragmented and fallen bodies of the Giants scattered across the landscape.
Dante sees the symbolic marks of Niobe, Saul, and Arachne along the path.
Dante observes the signs of Rehoboam and the tragic fate of Sennacherib's family.

scenes.scene_name: The remnants of war and ruin
scenes.summary: Dante sees the marks of the Assyrian retreat and the total destruction of Troy.
Dante marvels at how lifelike and artistically profound the shadows and their attributes appear.
Virgil rebukes Dante's downward gaze and commands him to lift his head as they progress.
Virgil points out an angel descending toward them to assist in their ascent.
A beautiful, white-clad angel arrives and invites the travelers to climb the approaching stairs.
The travelers begin a steep ascent that is compared to climbing the difficult stairs of Florence.
As they ascend, the travelers hear blessed voices that contrast sharply with the screams of Hell.

scenes.scene_name: The mystery of the weightless ascent
scenes.summary: Dante asks why his burden feels lighter, and Virgil responds with a cryptic prophecy regarding the end of their journey.
Dante feels something on his forehead and discovers six mysterious letters inscribed there, causing Virgil to smile.
